description_long: This is a medium-sized deciduous tree with a slender, slightly curved trunk that displays a mottled, grayish bark texture. The trunk branches out irregularly into several main limbs, each supporting clusters of green, broad leaves. The foliage is moderately dense, allowing some light to filter through, and the leaves are arranged in an alternate pattern along the branches. The tree's canopy is asymmetrical, with more foliage concentrated on one side, giving it a natural, windswept appearance. No visible roots extend above the ground, and the base of the trunk is relatively narrow compared to the overall height. This tree would be well-suited for placement in urban parks, sidewalks, or residential green spaces.
description: A slender, medium-sized deciduous tree with a curved trunk, mottled bark, and moderately dense clusters of green broad leaves. The canopy is asymmetrical and the trunk is narrow at the base.
description_view_0: The tree is seen with its trunk curving to the right and two main clusters of foliage, one lower and one higher, with visible gaps between branches.
description_view_1: This view shows the tree's foliage as more vertically aligned, with the trunk mostly obscured by the dense central cluster of leaves.
description_view_2: The trunk appears to lean left, with the foliage forming a more horizontal spread and the lower trunk more visible.
description_view_3: The tree is viewed head-on, showing the trunk straight up the center and the canopy forming a roughly oval shape with moderate density.
